name: Infosec & DC443 Meetup
description:
SUMMARY:
The DEF CON Group 443, also known as DC443, is an interest group designed to discuss general hacking and its culture, information security (InfoSec), and related topics. Our group’s interests include InfoSec, SDR, Hackathons, Speaker Events (MiniCons), Capture The Flags (CTFs), Binary Reversing/Exploitation, Ham Radio, Lock Picking, Micro Electronics, Web Development, etc.

We plan to meet every other week on Fridays at Unallocated space. Meetings can include Talks, projects, trivia nights, workshops, or contests and CTFs.

Website: https://dc443.org
Twitter: https://twitter.com/dc443_

GOAL: Learn about new and existing interests and grow the Unallocated Space community.

THINGS TO BRING: Yourself and friends

SKILL LEVEL:
Any

PRESENTER: Adam (Digital Tinker)

name: Casual Coding, Chat, & Coffee
description:
Join us for an informal gathering of Python enthusiasts at ZiPani, where we'll blend our love for coding with the rich aroma of coffee! Whether you're a seasoned developer or just starting out, this meetup is designed for everyone who enjoys Python programming in a relaxed environment.

Plan:

* **Casual Coding:** Work on your personal projects, get help, or share your code with others.
* **Open Discussion:** From Python libraries to best practices, let's talk about what's on your mind.
* **Networking:** Meet other local Python developers, exchange ideas, and maybe find your next coding buddy or collaborator.

Why Attend?

* Learn something new or share your knowledge in a friendly atmosphere.
* Get real-time help with coding problems or brainstorm new ideas.
* Discover local job opportunities or side projects within the Python community.

No need for formal presentations or slides; this is all about conversation and collaboration. Feel free to come and go as your schedule allows.

name: [ONLINE] Game Dev Meetup (Monthly Meetup)
description:
ONLINE MEETUP USING DISCORD. (Discord link visible after RSVPing.)

We're meeting on Discord! The link to join the Baltimore Indie Game Devs discord server will be in your RSVP message.

Join us to discuss game development, talk shop, and bounce ideas. Anyone with an interest in game design and development is welcome, from beginners to veteran professionals.

If you're working on a game, you're welcome to show it and ask for feedback. There will be plenty of time to chat and talk shop, too.

name: Project Night
description:
Monday evenings, we will be open for Project Night - a night to work on, collaborate on, or brainstorm on whatever projects you're currently working on or interested in.
Don't have a project? Come see what others are working on and get involved or get ideas. If you have an concept for a group project, bring your ideas and seek out others to join you.

Project Night is a great opportunity to make progress on your projects as a regularly scheduled event. Anyone is welcome to work on projects any time the space is open, but a dedicated night--just for this--is really fantastic.

Please check out our COVID restrictions at: https://wiki.unallocatedspace.org/wiki/index.php?title=COVID-19_Reopening_Plan

Key: Bowie
Free and open to the public

name: Grief & Hope for People Impacted by Federal Changes
description: Join peers in learning how to manage grief & foster hope through indigenous knowledge and mental health

name: Technical Readiness Level for Founders
description:
**RSVPs are required due to venue safety precautions. Anyone that's not on the RSVP list by July 7th 7pm will not be able to attend the event.**

**Agenda:**
6pm: Networking session
7pm: Speaker begins and Q&A afterwards

Are you a founder or in the startup scene, and wondering if you're ready to go in front of investors? In this event, seasoned startup founder and investor Jonathan Jenkins will be sharing his experience from both perspectives and answer any questions you may have.

Jonathan Jenkins is a Marine Corps veteran turned seasoned business and technology leader, with over 30 years of experience as an executive, inventor, coach, and public speaker focused on helping organizations enhance their leadership and innovation. He currently serves as Strategic Growth Advisor, mentor to many medium to large scale companies, and as well as a VC advisor.

During this event, Jonathan will be pulling back the curtain on how investment process works and share applicable tips with startup founders. If you're interested in learning more about the investment process, join us on July 8th in Northeastern University's Rosslyn location.

description: Project Night at Prefect

name: Old Dominion Grill and Sushi (2nd Wed Dinner)
description:

Come on out for some fun and food. This is a great chance to chat about Python with other people interested in the language.

Next month we'll have another Python presentation in store.

name: [IN-PERSON/ONLINE] Game Dev Meetup (Elkridge Branch!)
description:
**IN-PERSON + ONLINE**

*In-person is at the **Elkridge Branch Library** in the* *Grist Mill Room.*

Join us **IN-PERSON** or ONLINE. Anyone with an interest in game design and development is welcome, from beginners to veteran professionals.

We will be meeting in the Grist Mill Room at the Elkridge Branch Library (6540 Washington Blvd, Elkridge, MD 21075). (Not an official Howard County Library System event.)

If you want to attend virtually, the Discord server link is provided in the main monthly (1st of the month) meetup details. An organizer will ensure that a laptop is provided on-site to connect the Discord attendees with those physically on-site. It takes us a few minutes to set up the projector, so hold tight when you join the Discord voice chat channel. We'll be there soon!

name: The #BaltimoreTech Happy Hour!
description:
This month, we will be meeting at Raw & Refined on Canton's waterfront for networking and happy hour. This casual, recurring happy hour is for both new and seasoned Baltimore technologists interested in connecting with fellow tech enthusiasts and a diverse group of awesome people.

Stay tuned on all Baltimore Tech Meetups on our [Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/company/baltimore-tech-meetup/) or on Slack - [Baltimore Tech Slack](https://baltimoretechslack.com/) and our **new** email list at [https://www.baltimoretechmeetup.com/](https://www.baltimoretechmeetup.com/)

Date: July 24th 2025
Location: Raw & Refined - 2723 Lighthouse Point E, Baltimore, MD 21224
Time: 5:30pm to 7:30 pm

\#\#\# A few logistical details:

* We start at 5:30 PM. It usually lasts for a few hours - come and go when works for you.
* There is on street parking and a paid parking lot next to the venue
* By attending, all participants commit to upholding and being subject to our[ Code of Conduct](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ctYtmK0_q16rm7Vk3hdBkC6o_4MQZ_148bEKLR9JoCE/edit?usp=sharing).
* By attending, participants also acknowledge and grant permission for the use of photographs they may appear in for promotional material by the organizers or sponsors. If you would like to rescind this permission, please contact us directly.

name: How Human-Centric Identity Strategy Unlocks DevOps Potential
description:
Abstract:
Modern technology decisions often occur at the intersection of three critical domains: Identity Security, User Experience, and DevOps. While many organizations may treat these as separate concerns with competing priorities, the most successful systems are designed at their convergence, where security enhances rather than hinders user experience, and where operational excellence enables both protection and satisfaction.
As an identity strategist who works at this three-way intersection daily, I'll share what becomes visible when you stop optimizing for individual domains and start optimizing for their convergence.
Audience: DevOps engineers, security professionals, UX practitioners, and technical leaders interested in understanding how these critical domains intersect and amplify each other in modern system design.

Marcus ("Mr. Identity Security") Wells

name: Design for Planets
description:
We’ll gather at Common Ground Café (near Penn Station) for:

1. Interactive discussion about UX’s role in urban infrastructure, mobility, and community resilience
2. Hands-on system mapping using 3D-printed toy blocks

You don’t need to be an expert—just bring curiosity, an open mind, and a willingness to collaborate!

Drop in anytime between 11 and 1 for a hands-on design lab—stay as long or as little as you like.

About me: I've spent about a decade in US and Product design before starting a masters in Engineering at VCU (Richmond). Taking the train up to meet your awesome folks!

Image made with ChatGPT, after an hour or so of prompting.

name: [Virtual] Hackthebox Working Group
description:
This event is changing! Moving forward we'll be using Discord instead of Zoom. Please join us here: https://discord.gg/sF8P8fY5Nd

In the future we're also moving to a new meetup group. You can join that here: https://www.meetup.com/hack-the-box-meetup-baltimore/

We'll be hanging out and working on hackthebox.eu machines. We'll pick a machine to go after at the beginning of the evening and work together to hack into it. Please follow all rules listed here: https://www.hackthebox.eu/home/rules.

Collaboration is allowed, spoiling boxes and sharing flags is not.

name: Queers and Beers (and Other Delightful Drinks!)
description:
Queers and Beers (and Other Delightful Drinks!)

Calling all people of the rainbow! Want to learn more about our hacker/maker space while meeting new people? Come to Queers and Beers (and Other Delightful Drinks)!

This meetup is open to all queer/LGBTQIA2S+ people whether you've been to Unallocated before or not.

The tone will be casual: come as you are and be prepared to socialize. Bring drinks and snacks for yourself or to share. There will be some craft supplies if you want to make something.

THINGS TO BRING: Yourself, some snacks, and a tasty beverage (please be responsible and 21+ if you plan to bring alcohol)

PRESENTER: Thea

name: Detecting Code Vulnerabilities Using Python with AI and LLMs (2nd Wed Talk)
description:
Let's take a look at how easy detecting vulnerabilities in code can be using the latest and greatest technologies, including scale challenges and costs.

Also, we're going to have another "after hour." After the presentation is over, anyone who wants to stay and do a bit of coding or chatting is welcome to hang out. Think of it like a mini open workshop.

Don't have a project? Come see what others are working on and get involved or get ideas. If you have an concept for a group project, bring your ideas and seek out others to join you.

Project Night is a great opportunity to make progress on your projects as a regularly scheduled event. Anyone is welcome to work on projects any time the space is open, but a dedicated night--just for this--is really fantastic.

Please check out our COVID restrictions at: https://wiki.unallocatedspace.org/wiki/index.php?title=COVID-19_Reopening_Plan

name: Infosec & DC443 Meetup
description:
SUMMARY:
The DEF CON Group 443, also known as DC443, is an interest group designed to discuss general hacking and its culture, information security (InfoSec), and related topics. Our group’s interests include InfoSec, SDR, Hackathons, Speaker Events (MiniCons), Capture The Flags (CTFs), Binary Reversing/Exploitation, Ham Radio, Lock Picking, Micro Electronics, Web Development, etc.

We plan to meet every other week on Fridays at Unallocated space. Meetings can include Talks, projects, trivia nights, workshops, or contests and CTFs.

Website: https://dc443.org
Twitter: https://twitter.com/dc443_

GOAL: Learn about new and existing interests and grow the Unallocated Space community.

THINGS TO BRING: Yourself and friends

SKILL LEVEL:
Any

PRESENTER: Adam (Digital Tinker)

name: Casual Coding, Chat, & Coffee
description:
Join us for an informal gathering of Python enthusiasts at ZiPani, where we'll blend our love for coding with the rich aroma of coffee! Whether you're a seasoned developer or just starting out, this meetup is designed for everyone who enjoys Python programming in a relaxed environment.

Plan:

* **Casual Coding:** Work on your personal projects, get help, or share your code with others.
* **Open Discussion:** From Python libraries to best practices, let's talk about what's on your mind.
* **Networking:** Meet other local Python developers, exchange ideas, and maybe find your next coding buddy or collaborator.

Why Attend?

* Learn something new or share your knowledge in a friendly atmosphere.
* Get real-time help with coding problems or brainstorm new ideas.
* Discover local job opportunities or side projects within the Python community.

No need for formal presentations or slides; this is all about conversation and collaboration. Feel free to come and go as your schedule allows.

name: Accelerator Showcase
description:
Join the fellows of the StarTUp Accelerator as they pitch their ventures to campus,
                                 community and business leaders.

name: All Levels: Online Ask Me Anything WordPress!
description:
Ask Us Anything WordPress!

Register for it by filling out this form: https://forms.gle/Th6AZUbS2GoiCEJz5

This is a virtual meetup on Zoom for you to ask your WordPress questions and help you fix problems with your website.

The answerer for this meetup will be Gen Herres, owner of Anphira Web Design & Development, and a WordPress veteran of over a decade. She's built and remodeled hundreds of WordPress sites.

Don't have a question? You can still come and see what others ask - you might learn something!

We're asking you to submit questions in advance (please use the form at https://forms.gle/Th6AZUbS2GoiCEJz5) so that we can gather common ones together to answer them. But if you'd like to share your screen when it's your turn during the meetup, just let us know on the registration form!

You'll be muted on entry, we'll be using the chat to queue people up/ask follow-on questions.

The meetup will run 11am ET until 12pm ET or until we finish up the questions, whichever comes first. If you pre-submitted your question and are not present, then your question will be skipped.

Meetup is scheduled to be recorded. Recordings are posted about 2 weeks after the meetup and are provided courtesy of our sponsor, [Obee Digital Consulting](https://obeedigitalconsulting.com/).

How to get notified about the recording: either subscribe to the [youtube channel](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLoT0wv53Bj-BGfD8fPhMRC3qTtPMZHioI), RSVP "yes" to the meetup, or [join the mailing list](https://share.mailercloud.com/form?id=a0NzdUAxMTI1OUAwMDAwMA==).

name: Open House
description:
Open House Night - Come join us and check out what UAS is all about. Keyholders and members will be available to answer questions, help with projects, and give tours. If you've never been to UAS before, this is a great time to check us out. We host a weekly open house to help introduce new people to the space and some of the various interests and participants. Come on by, and check out the space and some of the fun projects we are working on.

Hosting Keyholder: Whitney

name: [Virtual] Hackthebox Working Group
description:
This event is changing! Moving forward we'll be using Discord instead of Zoom. Please join us here: https://discord.gg/sF8P8fY5Nd

In the future we're also moving to a new meetup group. You can join that here: https://www.meetup.com/hack-the-box-meetup-baltimore/

We'll be hanging out and working on hackthebox.eu machines. We'll pick a machine to go after at the beginning of the evening and work together to hack into it. Please follow all rules listed here: https://www.hackthebox.eu/home/rules.

Collaboration is allowed, spoiling boxes and sharing flags is not.

name: Queers and Beers (and Other Delightful Drinks!)
description:
Queers and Beers (and Other Delightful Drinks!)

Calling all people of the rainbow! Want to learn more about our hacker/maker space while meeting new people? Come to Queers and Beers (and Other Delightful Drinks)!

This meetup is open to all queer/LGBTQIA2S+ people whether you've been to Unallocated before or not.

The tone will be casual: come as you are and be prepared to socialize. Bring drinks and snacks for yourself or to share. There will be some craft supplies if you want to make something.

THINGS TO BRING: Yourself, some snacks, and a tasty beverage (please be responsible and 21+ if you plan to bring alcohol)

name: Calling All Halloween Hooligans! Build a Haunted Halloween House!
description:
Spooky season is here! Engage your mind by blending art and engineering to construct your very own haunted house to keep. We’ll be using sturdy milk cartons, icing as our construction glue, and sweet and savory treats and candies. All materials provided, while they last. Dress for a mess! 
This program is for children between the ages of 8 and 17 years old. Children under the age of 12 MUST be accompanied by an adult. This is a STEAM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Art and Math) Program.

name: St. Mary's STEAM Festival
description:
St. Mary’s County Public Schools will host their 10th annual STEAM Festival on Saturday, October 25, 2025 at the St. Mary’s County Fairgrounds. Festivities will run from 10:00 am to 2:00 pm and will feature fun, food, and phenomena! 
Each year, the state designates a month-long celebration of all things science, technology, engineering, and math. This STEM-related exploration takes place through in person festivals and online events. Our annual STEAM Festival is set up in a fair-style manner, where attendees can engage with exhibitors at their own pace.

description:
SUMMARY:
The DEF CON Group 443, also known as DC443, is an interest group designed to discuss general hacking and its culture, information security (InfoSec), and related topics. Our group’s interests include InfoSec, SDR, Hackathons, Speaker Events (MiniCons), Capture The Flags (CTFs), Binary Reversing/Exploitation, Ham Radio, Lock Picking, Micro Electronics, Web Development, etc.

We plan to meet every other week on Fridays at Unallocated space. Meetings can include Talks, projects, trivia nights, workshops, or contests and CTFs.

Website: https://dc443.org
Twitter: https://twitter.com/dc443_

GOAL: Learn about new and existing interests and grow the Unallocated Space community.

THINGS TO BRING: Yourself and friends

SKILL LEVEL:
Any

PRESENTER: Adam (Digital Tinker)

name: Project Night
description:
Monday evenings, we will be open for Project Night - a night to work on, collaborate on, or brainstorm on whatever projects you're currently working on or interested in.
Don't have a project? Come see what others are working on and get involved or get ideas. If you have an concept for a group project, bring your ideas and seek out others to join you.

Project Night is a great opportunity to make progress on your projects as a regularly scheduled event. Anyone is welcome to work on projects any time the space is open, but a dedicated night--just for this--is really fantastic.

Please check out our COVID restrictions at: https://wiki.unallocatedspace.org/wiki/index.php?title=COVID-19_Reopening_Plan

Key: Bowie
Free and open to the public

name: Open House
description:
Open House Night - Come join us and check out what UAS is all about. Keyholders and members will be available to answer questions, help with projects, and give tours. If you've never been to UAS before, this is a great time to check us out. We host a weekly open house to help introduce new people to the space and some of the various interests and participants. Come on by, and check out the space and some of the fun projects we are working on.

Hosting Keyholder: Whitney

name: [Virtual] Hackthebox Working Group
description:
This event is changing! Moving forward we'll be using Discord instead of Zoom. Please join us here: https://discord.gg/sF8P8fY5Nd

In the future we're also moving to a new meetup group. You can join that here: https://www.meetup.com/hack-the-box-meetup-baltimore/

We'll be hanging out and working on hackthebox.eu machines. We'll pick a machine to go after at the beginning of the evening and work together to hack into it. Please follow all rules listed here: https://www.hackthebox.eu/home/rules.

Collaboration is allowed, spoiling boxes and sharing flags is not.

Key: Bowie
Free and open to the public

name: Queers and Beers (and Other Delightful Drinks!)
description:
Queers and Beers (and Other Delightful Drinks!)

Calling all people of the rainbow! Want to learn more about our hacker/maker space while meeting new people? Come to Queers and Beers (and Other Delightful Drinks)!

This meetup is open to all queer/LGBTQIA2S+ people whether you've been to Unallocated before or not.

The tone will be casual: come as you are and be prepared to socialize. Bring drinks and snacks for yourself or to share. There will be some craft supplies if you want to make something.

THINGS TO BRING: Yourself, some snacks, and a tasty beverage (please be responsible and 21+ if you plan to bring alcohol)

PRESENTER: Thea

name: Locksport - MD TOOOL
description:
SUMMARY: Locksport @ UAS.

GOAL: Learn how locks work and how to pick them.

THINGS TO BRING: just yourself or locks & tools.

SKILL LEVEL: all

PRESENTER: MacGnG

https://www.unallocatedspace.org/events/locksport-md-toool/
